title: Testing & CI Automation
goal: Establish comprehensive automated testing suites and CI/CD pipeline to ensure quality, performance, and accessibility for the ASL translation app, preventing regressions and supporting continuous development.
scope: Implement unit tests for Flutter (widgets, BLoC logic), backend (API routes, models), and ML (inference accuracy).
Add integration tests for end-to-end flows (auth, translation pipeline).
Include UI tests (e.g., Flutter integration tests) for responsive layout and animations.
Add accessibility tests for deaf users (screen reader compatibility, high contrast).
Set up performance tests for ML inference (FPS benchmarks) and backend (load testing).
Configure CI pipeline (GitHub Actions) to run builds, tests, linting (Flutter analyze, ESLint), and static analysis on pushes/PRs.
Add code coverage reporting and quality gates (e.g., minimum coverage thresholds).
deliverables: Test suites: unit (Jest/Mocha for backend, Flutter test), integration, UI, accessibility.
CI config (.github/workflows/) with jobs for build, test, lint, and deploy previews.
Documentation on running tests locally and interpreting CI results.
Audit report and bug log for this phase.

examples.unit_test_backend: const request = require('supertest'); describe('Auth', () => { it('should login', async () => { const res = await request(app).post('/api/auth/login').send({email, password}); expect(res.status).toBe(200); }); });
examples.flutter_unit_test: test('HomeBloc emits loaded state', () { bloc.add(LoadHomeEvent()); expectLater(bloc, emits(HomeLoadedState('message'))); });
examples.ci_yaml:
name: CI
on: [push]
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2
      - run: flutter test
      - run: npm test
examples.accessibility_test: Use axe-core for web; for mobile, check contrast ratios manually.

must_verify: All test suites run successfully with high coverage (>80%).
CI pipeline triggers correctly and fails on regressions.
Linting/static analysis passes without major issues.
Performance tests meet targets (e.g., 60 FPS ML inference).
Accessibility features are tested and compliant.
Flaky tests or coverage gaps are documented and addressed.
corrections.json is created with fixes for any identified issues.
